attacking_style: Opportunistic late attacker who strikes on descents or uphill finishes with 2-9km to go, particularly effective when exploiting tactical standoffs rather than pure climbing power.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support; relies on breakaway positioning and jersey math calculations rather than domestique-led control. Team deliberately avoids chase responsibility when holding virtual lead.
terrain_preference: Descents fromcat-2 summits and uphill finishes suit best; exposed on final sustained climbs where pure climbers can distance by 20+ seconds. Local course knowledge provides tactical advantage.
vulnerability: Physically vulnerable to cramping under duress; team cannot control races in adverse conditions (rain). Lacks firepower to defend GC when rivals intensify tactics or on sustained final climbs against pure climbers.
key_patterns: Takes red jersey from breakaways, using time gaps to avoid team chase duties (3 instances)
Prioritizes jersey mathematics over stage wins, engaging tactical standoffs on descents (2 instances)
Attacks late 2-9km to go on descents or uphill finishes, not early climbs (3 wins)
Loses 20+ seconds to top GC contenders on final sustained climbs when forced to defend (2 instances)
Track racing background enables cold tactical calculation even when physically compromised (cramping documented)
form_trajectory: neutral: Successfully contested red jersey in 2022 through tactical intelligence but lacks physical capacity to defend against top GC riders when they attack. Role remains breakaway opportunist rather than GC contender.
